title: Cracking the code: social data access and the DSA's impact on disinformation research
snippet: This article, co-authored by Fabio Giglietto and Massimo Terenzi, discusses the impact of the Digital Services Act (DSA) on social media research, particularly concerning disinformation, algorithmic bias, and content moderation. It highlights both the potential and limitations of the DSA in providing researchers with access to platform data.
description: This article discusses the impact of the Digital Services Act (DSA) on social media research, specifically concerning disinformation, algorithmic bias, and content moderation, and is co-authored by Fabio Giglietto. It explores the potential and limitations of the DSA in providing researchers with access to platform data.

title: DIGITAL SOCIAL NETWORK ANALYSIS - Lecturer: Fabio Giglietto - A.Y. 2025/2026 - Uniurb
snippet: This page describes the Digital Social Network Analysis course taught by Fabio Giglietto at the University of Urbino. It covers methodologies for analyzing digital social environments, data collection, statistical analysis, and network analysis using R and Gephi.
description: This is a course description for a Digital Social Network Analysis course taught by Fabio Giglietto at the University of Urbino, covering relevant methodologies and analysis techniques.

title: Who is behind vera.ai? Meet the people and teams that make up the vera.ai project. #8 Team of University of Urbino Carlo Bo
snippet: Fabio Giglietto leads the University of Urbino Carlo Bo team within the vera.ai project, coordinating Work Package 4 (WP4). The team focuses on discovering potentially problematic content in near-real-time by addressing coordinated sharing behavior using social media analysis and network science methods.
description: The web mention explicitly names Fabio Giglietto as the leader of the University of Urbino Carlo Bo team within the vera.ai project, focusing on coordinated sharing behavior using social media analysis and network science methods.
